summary: Provision new AWS Batch compute environments, job queues, and job definitions. Every stand-up routes to an engineer for review — day-2 operations on existing Batch resources land in a later wave.

operations.title: Provision a new Batch compute environment
operations.summary: Request a brand-new AWS Batch compute environment; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new managed compute environment as a complete specification: capacity type, size bounds, and network placement. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: compute_env_name
params.label: Compute environment name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like batch-fargate-env. Names the compute environment in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: env_type
params.label: Environment type
params.help: Always MANAGED — AWS provisions and scales the underlying capacity; written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: compute_type
params.label: Compute type
params.help: FARGATE and FARGATE_SPOT need no instance sizing below; EC2 and SPOT provision from the estate's own capacity.

params.name: max_vcpus
params.label: Maximum vCPUs
params.help: The most vCPUs this compute environment scales up to across all running jobs.

params.name: subnets
params.label: Subnets
params.help: Where job capacity launches. Pick subnets spanning at least two Availability Zones for resilience.

params.name: security_group_ids
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Leave blank to use the VPC default security group.

params.name: service_role
params.label: Batch service role
params.help: Leave blank to use the AWS-managed Batch service-linked role.

params.name: state
params.label: State
params.help: DISABLED stops the environment from accepting new job capacity until turned on.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this compute environment does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this compute environment.

decisions: EC2/SPOT instance sizing (instance_type, instance_role, launch template, allocation strategy) is an engineer decision — this form covers the common Fargate case
Placement group and Spot bid percentage are engineer follow-ups when the EC2/SPOT compute type is chosen

operations.title: Provision a new Batch job queue
operations.summary: Request a brand-new AWS Batch job queue; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new job queue as a complete specification: priority, state, and the compute environment it submits work to. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: queue_name
params.label: Queue name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like batch-default-queue. Names the queue in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: priority
params.label: Priority
params.help: Higher numbers are evaluated first when multiple queues share the same compute environment.

params.name: state
params.label: State
params.help: DISABLED stops the queue from accepting new job submissions until turned on.

params.name: compute_environment
params.label: Compute environment
params.help: The compute environment this queue submits jobs to. Create the compute environment first (batch-compute-environment-create) if none exists yet.

params.name: order
params.label: Order
params.help: The first (and only) compute environment in this queue's order — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this job queue does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this job queue.

decisions: A multi-environment fallback order (e.g. Fargate then Fargate Spot) beyond the single environment authored here is an engineer follow-up
A fair-share scheduling policy (scheduling_policy_arn) is an engineer decision — this form uses the default FIFO queue behavior

operations.title: Provision a new Batch job definition
operations.summary: Request a brand-new AWS Batch job definition; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new job definition as a complete specification: platform capability, retry and timeout behavior, and the container spec it runs. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block — including the container spec — to review and finish. Nothing is applied without review.

params.name: job_def_name
params.label: Job definition name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like nightly-report-job. Names the job definition in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: job_type
params.label: Job type
params.help: Always container — the common case; multi-node parallel and EKS job shapes are an engineer decision.

params.name: container_properties
params.label: Container properties
params.help: The JSON container spec — image, vCPUs, memory, command — an engineer authors this to match the workload's built image and resource needs.

params.name: platform_capability
params.label: Platform capability
params.help: Which compute environment platform this job definition can run on.

params.name: retry_attempts
params.label: Retry attempts
params.help: How many times a failed job attempt is retried before it is marked FAILED.

params.name: timeout_seconds
params.label: Timeout (seconds)
params.help: Jobs running longer than this are terminated. Leave blank for no timeout.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this job definition does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this job definition.

decisions: The container spec (image, vCPUs, memory, command) is an engineer decision, matched to the workload's built image
Multi-node parallel jobs (node_properties) and EKS jobs (eks_properties) are engineer decisions — this form covers the common single-container case
Exit-code-based retry rules (evaluate_on_exit) beyond a flat attempt count are an engineer follow-up
